member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager
member.summary: Manages system and custom accent colors and publishes them as DynamicResource brush keys aligned with Windows 11.
member.remarks: Call , , or after so theme-dependent primary/secondary/tertiary accents resolve correctly.

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.AccentColorChanged
member.summary: Occurs after accent ramp colors and application resources have been updated.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.#cctor
member.summary: Initializes static members of the ApplicationAccentColorManager class and subscribes once to the theme engine so is raised after every publish.
member.remarks: This static constructor is called automatically before any static members are accessed or any instances are created.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.EnsureInitialized
member.summary: Forces the static constructor to run, wiring the subscription before the first call. Called by before the engine fires its first publish so that the initial event is never missed.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColor
member.summary: Gets the current base system accent color (ARGB). Default is a Windows blue until runs.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColorLight1
member.summary: Gets the first light tint on the generated accent ramp. Default matches until the ramp is loaded.

member.summary: Gets the second light tint on the generated accent ramp.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColorLight3
member.summary: Gets the lightest tint on the generated accent ramp.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColorDark3
member.summary: Gets the darkest shade on the generated accent ramp.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColorSecondary
member.summary: Gets the secondary accent color used for layered emphasis.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.SystemAccentColorTertiary

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether Windows is configured to show accent color on title bars and window borders.

member.summary: Gets the active titlebar color (from DWM AccentColor or default gray).

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.TitleBarInactiveColor

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.WindowBorderColor
member.summary: Gets the window border color (titlebar active on Win11, blended on Win10).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.ApplySystemAccent
member.summary: Sets the accent intent to the live Windows accent palette and re-applies the current theme.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationAccentColorManager.ApplyApplicationAccent

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager
member.summary: Manages Fluence.Wpf theme resource dictionaries, accent coordination, and runtime theme changes.
member.remarks: delegates to the internal theme engine, which publishes one computed colors-plus-brushes dictionary at slot [0] and replaces it on every change.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.CurrentTheme
member.summary: Gets the currently requested theme (may be ).

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.CurrentBackdrop
member.summary: Gets the currently requested backdrop type.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.ResolvedTheme
member.summary: Gets the concrete theme (Light, Dark, or HighContrast) that was resolved and applied during the most recent theme pipeline run (that is, the last call to or to any apply method (, , or ), whichever ran last. When is , this reflects the OS theme at the time of that last pipeline run; it does not update automatically when the OS theme changes without a subsequent pipeline run. Before the first pipeline run, this property returns as the pre-initialization default.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.IsSystemInDarkMode
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the Windows system (window-chrome) color mode is currently Dark. Reflects the live registry value; independent of .

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.IsAppInDarkMode
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the Windows app color mode is currently Dark. Reflects the live registry value; independent of .

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.Changed
member.summary: Raised after a theme or accent change has been applied.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.Apply(Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationTheme,Fluence.Wpf.BackdropType,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the theme resource stack or applies a later theme change.
member.param: The requested application theme. Use to follow Windows app theme settings.
The requested window backdrop policy retained for consumers.
Accepted for signature compatibility; the single pipeline always rebuilds the computed dictionary using the current accent intent.
member.remarks: The first call seeds the three resource slots ([0] computed colors and brushes, [1] Typography, [2] Generic). Later calls replace the computed slot so DynamicResource consumers re-resolve.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationThemeManager.ApplySystemTheme
member.summary: Re-applies with to pick up system changes.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Automation.TextBlockAutomationPeer
member.summary: Exposes to UI Automation as a text element.

remarks.para: wraps a inside a template to support the Fluent typography ramp. Without this peer, WPF creates a generic peer that reports ControlType.Pane, placing a spurious container in the UIA tree and breaking AutomationProperties.LabeledBy relationships that expect ControlType.Text.
Only instances with an explicit are visible in the UIA control view. Instances without a name are excluded so decorative body-copy text is not announced; the name is never derived from automatically.

member.param: The control represented by this automation peer.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Automation.TextBlockAutomationPeer.#ctor(Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TextBlock)
member.summary: Exposes to UI Automation as a text element.

remarks.para: wraps a inside a template to support the Fluent typography ramp. Without this peer, WPF creates a generic peer that reports ControlType.Pane, placing a spurious container in the UIA tree and breaking AutomationProperties.LabeledBy relationships that expect ControlType.Text.
Only instances with an explicit are visible in the UIA control view. Instances without a name are excluded so decorative body-copy text is not announced; the name is never derived from automatically.

member.param: The control represented by this automation peer.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Automation.ToggleSplitButtonAutomationPeer
member.summary: Exposes to UI Automation with the Toggle pattern (primary half) and the ExpandCollapse pattern (flyout half). The Invoke pattern is deliberately not offered: WinUI exposes only Toggle and ExpandCollapse on its ToggleSplitButton peer, and an Invoke routed through the SplitButton peer would raise Click without toggling, diverging from a real primary-half click.
member.remarks: Initializes a new instance.
member.param: The control represented by this automation peer.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker
member.summary: A control that lets the user pick a color from a saturation/value spectrum, a hue slider, an optional alpha slider, a current/previous preview swatch row, and a text-entry area with an RGB/HSV representation selector, per-channel inputs, an alpha percentage input, and a hex input, mirroring the WinUI 3 ColorPicker. The picker keeps hue, saturation, value, and alpha as its internal source of truth so dragging across the grey axis does not accumulate RGB round-trip drift, the same approach WinUI uses.
member.remarks: Scope notes: WinUI's ColorSpectrumShape (the Ring spectrum), the ColorSpectrumComponents permutations, Orientation, and the Min/Max channel range properties are deliberately omitted. The spectrum is fixed to saturation on the x axis by value on the y axis at the selected hue, with hue on a horizontal slider serving as the third-dimension color slider. Channel and alpha text inputs commit live on every valid keystroke like WinUI; the hex input commits on Enter or focus loss, a deliberate deviation from WinUI's live hex commit.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.SpectrumSize
member.summary: Pixel size of the square saturation/value spectrum bitmap.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.SpectrumSmallStep
member.summary: Small step applied to saturation or value when the user presses an arrow key on the focused spectrum area. Matches a 1% increment on the 0-1 scale.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.SpectrumLargeStep
member.summary: Large step applied to saturation or value when the user presses PageUp or PageDown on the focused spectrum area. Matches a 10% increment on the 0-1 scale.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.#cctor

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the alpha channel can be edited. When (the default) the alpha slider row and the alpha text input collapse, the hex input parses and displays six digits, and turning the property off pins the picker's alpha back to 255. Programmatic assignments keep whatever alpha they carry.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the hex text input is shown. The input accepts #RRGGBB and #AARRGGBB (the leading # is optional) and commits on Enter or when keyboard focus leaves the box.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.IsMoreButtonVisible
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the text-entry area collapses behind a More/Less toggle button. When (the default) the text inputs are always visible and no toggle is shown; when the toggle appears and the text-entry area stays collapsed until it is checked, matching WinUI.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.IsColorChannelTextInputVisibleProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.IsColorChannelTextInputVisible
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the RGB/HSV representation selector and the per-channel text inputs are shown. The hex input is governed separately by and the alpha input by , matching WinUI. Channel input commits live on every valid keystroke; Enter or focus loss normalizes the text and restores it after invalid input.

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.ColorChanged
member.summary: Occurs after changes, whether through the spectrum, the sliders, the channel or hex text inputs, or a programmatic update. Channel and alpha text inputs commit live, so the event can fire once per keystroke while the user types (for example "1", "12", "120"), matching WinUI.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.CreateCheckerboardBrush
member.summary: Creates the frozen checkerboard brush rendered beneath translucent surfaces (the alpha slider track and the preview swatches).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.CreateHueRainbowBrush
member.summary: Creates the frozen hue rainbow brush for the hue slider track. The left edge is hue 0 and the right edge is hue 360 (which wraps back to red), matching the slider's Minimum 0 / Maximum 360 left-to-right orientation. Seven stops, one per 60 degree primary/secondary, all derived through so no literal colors appear here either.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.SetColorFromHsv(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Byte)
member.summary: Central funnel for every picker-driven change: clamps and stores the HSV + alpha model, publishes the resulting through (so bindings survive), and refreshes the visuals even when the RGB value is unchanged (the spectrum thumb and hue slider can move while the color stays the same, e.g. on the grey axis).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.SyncHsvFromColor(System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Re-derives the HSV model from an externally assigned color. Achromatic colors collapse hue (and black additionally collapses saturation) to zero in the RGB to HSV conversion; the previous component is retained in those cases so the spectrum thumb and hue slider do not jump while the color sits on the grey axis. WinUI's ColorPicker behaves the same way.
member.param: The color to synchronize from.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.UpdateSpectrumBitmap
member.summary: Regenerates the 256 x 256 saturation/value spectrum bitmap for the current hue. Per pixel this uses the standard HSV expansion channel = V * (255 - S * (255 - hueChannel)), so the only full conversion per regeneration is the pure hue color from . Skipped when the cached bitmap already matches the hue.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.UpdateVisuals
member.summary: Pushes the current HSV + alpha model into every visual: slider positions (guarded so their ValueChanged handlers do not feed back), the spectrum thumb, the alpha gradient, the preview swatches, and the hex text.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.UpdateChannelTextBoxes
member.summary: Rewrites the channel and alpha text boxes from the current model, skipping the group the user is typing in so the live per-keystroke commit never destroys the caret of the active box. RGB boxes display the channels exactly; HSV boxes round to integers for display only (the model keeps full precision); the alpha box displays a percentage with a trailing percent sign.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.UpdateSpectrumThumb
member.summary: Positions the selection ellipse over the spectrum (x = saturation, y = inverted value) and flips its ring between white and black based on the luminance of the color underneath, the same contrast rule WinUI applies to its selection ellipse.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.UpdateAlphaGradient
member.summary: Rebuilds the alpha slider's transparent-to-opaque gradient over the checkerboard so the track always previews the current hue/saturation/value.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.GetHexDisplayText
member.summary: Formats the current color for the hex input and the automation peer: #AARRGGBB when , otherwise #RRGGBB.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.ApplySpectrumPoint(System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: Maps a point inside the spectrum area to saturation (x, 0 to 1 left to right) and value (y, 1 to 0 top to bottom) and routes it through the central HSV funnel. Internal so the test suite can drive the drag math deterministically; the mouse handlers funnel through here.
member.param: The point within the spectrum area to apply.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.OnKeyDown(System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles arrow and page key presses when keyboard focus is on the spectrum area, adjusting saturation (Left/Right) or value (Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown) and routing through the HSV funnel so there is no RGB round-trip drift.
member.param: The key event arguments.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.CommitHexText
member.summary: Parses the hex box; valid input is published directly through the Color DP (no HSV round trip, so the typed RGB value is preserved exactly), invalid input reverts the text to the current color.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.OnRgbTextBoxTextChanged(System.Object,System.Windows.Controls.TextChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Commits a red, green, or blue channel edit live. The target color is rebuilt from the current plus the parsed channel and published directly through the Color DP, like the hex commit, so the typed RGB value is preserved exactly; 's hue/saturation retention keeps the spectrum thumb stable on greys. Invalid text is a no-op until Enter or focus loss restores it.
member.param: The text box that raised the event.
Event data for the text-changed event.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.OnHsvTextBoxTextChanged(System.Object,System.Windows.Controls.TextChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Commits a hue, saturation, or value channel edit live. Only the edited component is replaced, going straight through the HSV funnel so the untouched components keep their full precision (deliberately better than WinUI, which re-reads all three boxes and quantizes the untouched components to integers). Hue accepts 0 to 360 because the picker's model and slider use 360 inclusive.
member.param: The text box that raised the event.
Event data for the text-changed event.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.OnAlphaTextBoxTextChanged(System.Object,System.Windows.Controls.TextChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Commits an alpha percentage edit live. The box accepts 0 to 100 with an optional trailing percent sign (auto-appended on normalize), mapped to the 0 to 255 alpha byte, like WinUI's opacity input.
member.param: The text box that raised the event.
Event data for the text-changed event.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.TryParseChannelValue(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Strictly parses a non-negative invariant integer between zero and inclusive. Signs, group separators, and embedded whitespace are rejected; surrounding whitespace is trimmed.
member.param: The text to parse.
The inclusive upper bound for the parsed value.
The parsed integer if successful; otherwise, zero.
member.returns: if the text was successfully parsed and is within range; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.TryParseAlphaPercent(System.String,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Parses the alpha box text as a 0 to 100 percentage, tolerating one trailing percent sign.
member.param: The text to parse.
The parsed percentage value (0 to 100) if successful; otherwise, zero.
member.returns: if the text was successfully parsed as a valid percentage; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ColorPicker.TryParseHexColor(System.String,System.Windows.Media.Color@)
member.summary: Parses #RRGGBB / #AARRGGBB (leading # optional). Six-digit input is treated as fully opaque.
member.param: The hex color string to parse.
The parsed color if successful; otherwise, .
member.returns: if the hex color string was successfully parsed; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyout
member.summary: Represents a flyout that shows a horizontal bar of primary commands with an optional expandable overflow menu of secondary commands, mirroring the WinUI 3 CommandBarFlyout control.
member.remarks: Commands are usually instances. Clicking a command inside the flyout runs its normal Click handling and then dismisses the flyout, matching the WinUI behavior. The flyout participates in the full contract: it can be opened via or attached to an element with and opened via . The WinUI AlwaysExpanded option is omitted for v1; the flyout always opens collapsed and the overflow menu is toggled by the presenter's more button.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyout.PrimaryCommands
member.summary: Gets the commands shown in the always-visible horizontal primary bar.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyout.SecondaryCommands
member.summary: Gets the commands shown in the expandable overflow menu below the primary bar. The presenter's more button is only visible while this collection is non-empty.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyout.CreatePresenter
member.summary: Creates (or returns the cached) bound to this flyout's and .
member.returns: The presenter hosting the command bar.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyout._presenter
member.summary: The cached presenter instance reused across show and hide cycles.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.CommandBarFlyoutPresenter
member.summary: Displays the commands of a on the canonical Fluent flyout surface: a horizontal primary bar, a more button shown while secondary commands exist, and a collapsible overflow menu below the bar toggled via . The themed template lives in Themes/Controls/CommandBarFlyout.xaml.
member.remarks: Clicking any hosted in the presenter (other than the more button) dismisses the owning flyout after the button's normal Click handling, matching the WinUI command-dismiss behavior. The WinUI AlwaysExpanded option is omitted for v1, so the overflow collapses again whenever the flyout closes.

member.summary: Initializes static members of the class and overrides the default style metadata so the themed template in Generic.xaml applies.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog
member.summary: A modal dialog with a title area, arbitrary body content, and up to three command buttons, mirroring the WinUI 3 ContentDialog control. While open the dialog sits above a smoke layer that dims and blocks everything behind it: over a window that exposes a full-window overlay host (a FluenceWindow PART_DialogOverlayHost) the smoke covers the entire window, title bar included; over a plain window it is hosted in the content adorner layer. A tunneling input guard additionally blocks any press outside the dialog. The owner's visual tree is never restructured. The body uses the inherited and .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class. The dialog starts collapsed so a dialog declared in window XAML renders nothing inline at rest; it becomes visible while it is hosted in its modal overlay during and collapses again when it closes. SetCurrentValue keeps an explicit consumer-set authoritative.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.ShowAsync
member.summary: Shows the dialog modally over the active window (or the application main window) and returns a task that completes with the once the dialog closes. While open, a smoke layer dims the owner window content and blocks mouse input, Tab navigation is trapped inside the dialog, Escape dismisses as if the close button were invoked, and Enter invokes . Must be called on the dialog's dispatcher thread.
member.returns: A task that completes with the dialog result when the dialog closes.
member.exception: The dialog is already open, no owner window could be resolved, the owner window has no content root, or no adorner layer exists above the owner window content.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.Hide
member.summary: Closes the dialog with . Does nothing when the dialog is not open.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.OnKeyDown(System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.remarks: The Enter/ shortcut runs on the bubbling key event (not the tunneling preview) so focused body controls that consume Enter themselves, such as an AcceptsReturn TextBox or an open ComboBox, win over the default button.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.OnOwnerPreviewMouseDown(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.MouseButtonEventArgs)
member.summary: Swallows tunneling mouse presses anywhere in the owner window that do not originate inside the dialog, so chrome outside the content adorner (such as a title-bar search box) cannot be clicked or focused while the dialog is modal.
member.param: The source of the event.
The mouse button event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.OnOwnerPreviewKeyDown(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.summary: Swallows tunneling key input anywhere in the owner window that does not originate inside the dialog, mirroring , so chrome that still holds keyboard focus (such as a title-bar search box) cannot be typed into while the dialog is modal. Key input sourced inside the dialog is left alone so the dialog's own Tab cycle and key handling keep working.
member.param: The source of the event.
The key event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.OnOwnerClosed(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Closes the dialog with when the owner window closes while the dialog is open, so the pending task always completes.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.BeginOpenAnimation
member.summary: Plays the WinUI dialog entrance on the overlay-hosted dialog surface, mirroring the DialogShowing visual transition keyframes: opacity rises 0 to 1 linearly over the faster motion duration while the scale settles 1.05 to 1.0 around the center over the normal motion duration on the decelerating Fluent key spline. The animations use with base values equal to the end values, so nothing stays animated once the entrance completes.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.CreateOpenScaleAnimation
member.summary: Builds one axis of the entrance scale animation: 1.05 to 1.0 over the normal motion duration on the decelerating Fluent key spline (see for the WinUI keyframe source and the mirrored Typography.xaml motion tokens).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.IsWithinDialog(System.Windows.DependencyObject)
member.summary: Walks the visual (and, for non-visual sources, logical) tree from upward to determine whether it sits inside this dialog.
member.param: The starting point for the tree walk.
member.returns: True if the source is within the dialog; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.ResolveOwnerWindow
member.summary: Resolves the window that hosts the modal overlay: the active window first, then the application main window.
member.exception: Thrown when no active or main window can be resolved.

member.summary: Moves keyboard focus to the when one is available, otherwise to the dialog itself, so the Tab cycle starts inside the dialog.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.AnnounceLiveRegion
member.summary: Raises on this dialog's automation peer so Narrator announces the dialog by its (the peer name) the moment it opens. This is the net472-safe substitute for the .NET Framework 4.8 AutomationProperties.IsDialog announcement, paired with the assertive live setting declared in the static constructor. Uses only net472-safe APIs (no RaiseNotificationEvent).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.CloseDialog(Fluence.Wpf.ContentDialogResult)
member.summary: Removes the overlay adorner, restores the previously focused element, completes the pending task with , and raises .
member.param: The result to close the dialog with.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.BuildOverlayRoot
member.summary: Builds the overlay layout root: a smoke layer that dims and blocks everything behind it, with the dialog centered on top and Tab navigation trapped inside. A dialog declared in window XAML is detached from its declared parent first and made visible only while it is overlay-hosted.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ContentDialog.DetachFromParent(System.Windows.FrameworkElement)
member.summary: Detaches from its current parent so it can join the overlay layout root. The logical parent is preferred because it owns the content slot (a ContentControl's content reports a ContentPresenter as its visual parent); the visual parent is the fallback for template-generated hosts. Unsupported parents fail fast instead of letting the overlay grid throw WPF's generic re-parenting error.
member.param: The element to detach.
member.exception: The parent is not a , a (which includes Border), or a .

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker
member.summary: A control that lets the user pick a calendar date from day, month, and year selector columns hosted in a light-dismiss flyout, mirroring the WinUI 3 DatePicker. The always-visible field is a button-styled row showing the selected day, month name, and year ordered by the current culture's short date pattern; the flyout commits the pending column selection through its accept button and discards it through cancel.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.GetCultureOrderedFields(System.Globalization.DateTimeFormatInfo)
member.summary: Returns day, month, and year in the order they first occur in the current culture's short date pattern, so the field segments and selector columns read naturally (for example month-day-year for en-US, day-month-year for en-GB). Quoted literals inside exotic patterns are not skipped; for short date patterns they do not contain the d/M/y specifier characters in practice.
member.param: The to evaluate.
member.returns: A list of values in the order they appear in the short date pattern.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.FormatSegment(Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.DateField,System.DateTime,System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Formats one field of for display: the day and year as culture-formatted numbers, the month as the culture's full Gregorian month name (see ).
member.param: The field to format.
The date to format.
The culture to use for formatting.
member.returns: The formatted string for the specified field.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.GetGregorianFormat(System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Returns the culture's date format pinned to the Gregorian calendar so month names match the Gregorian day/year math the control performs. Cultures whose default calendar is non-Gregorian (for example Um Al Qura) would otherwise pair Gregorian numbers with the names of a different calendar. Falls back to the invariant format for the rare culture without an optional Gregorian calendar.
member.param: The culture to evaluate.
member.returns: The pinned to the Gregorian calendar.

member.summary: Returns the WinUI 3 star-width weight for a field column (78* for day and year, 132* for the wider month column).
member.param: The field to evaluate.
member.returns: The star-width weight for the specified field.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.IsWithinLightDismissReopenLockout
member.summary: Returns whether a flyout-button click arrived within the short lockout after a light dismiss. Clicking the open field light-dismisses the StaysOpen=false popup on the mouse press, and without this guard the same click would immediately reopen it, so the field could never close its own flyout.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.OnPopupClosed(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Records when the popup closes through a light dismiss (any close that did not run through ), arming the reopen lockout consumed by . Accept, cancel, and Escape closes do not arm it, so programmatic close-and-reopen flows stay instant.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.DatePicker.OnPopupPreviewKeyDown(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles flyout keyboard interaction at the popup root: Escape discards the pending column selection (the cancel-button path) and Enter commits it (the accept-button path). Enter is left alone while a flyout command button has keyboard focus so the button's native click handling wins.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.summary: Fills the three selector columns from (or today when unset, clamped into the .. range) and highlights the matching items. The columns are plain scrollable lists; WinUI's infinitely looping selectors are a deliberate v1 omission.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow
member.summary: A top-level window that recreates the Windows 11 Fluent / WinUI 3 chrome on WPF: a DWM system backdrop (Mica, Acrylic, or Tabbed), rounded corners, an extendable title bar, and custom caption buttons that integrate with the Windows 11 snap-layout flyout.
member.remarks: The window collapses the native non-client frame through and drives every caption interaction (drag, resize, snap-layout hover, maximize/restore) from a Win32 message hook so the custom chrome stays authoritative. Theme, accent, and backdrop are applied directly to the HWND via DWM attributes and kept in sync with the shared theme managers for the lifetime of the realised window.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.OnSizeChangedForSizeToContent(System.Object,System.Windows.SizeChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: handler that re-runs the SizeToContent client-area fill on every size change while is active.
member.param: The event source.
The size-changed payload (unused).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.FillClientAreaForSizeToContent
member.summary: Forces the template root visual to fill the realised client area when is active.
member.remarks:
A sizes its HWND to the latest content-desired size, but on a SizeToContent-driven resize the root visual's arrange lags one layout pass behind the new client size: the HWND (and / ) already reflect the grown size while the template root Border is still arranged to the previous, smaller desired size. The gap reads as a rounded accent border floating inside the DWM border (set via DWMWA_BORDER_COLOR) on every edge, because the template border and the DWM border no longer coincide. An interactive resize hides it because it ends with a real WM_SIZE that re-arranges the content; a SizeToContent first paint or auto-grow never produces that WM_SIZE.
The correction directly arranges the single visual child to a rect of the window's current x (which equal the client area in DIPs), reproducing the re-arrange a real WM_SIZE would trigger without freezing - so the window still grows when its content grows and stays single-bordered after growing. The SizeToContent != Manual guard makes it a no-op for fixed-size windows, which already render with the borders coincident, and a re-entrancy guard prevents the child arrange from recursing through .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.UpdateCaptionButtons
member.summary: Recomputes the visibility and enabled state of all four caption buttons by blending the baselines (derived from and ) with any explicitly-set caption-chrome DP overrides, then reflows the button slots so collapsed buttons leave no gap.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.UpdateCaptionButtonSlots(System.Windows.Visibility,System.Windows.Visibility,System.Windows.Visibility,System.Windows.Visibility)
member.summary: Re-assigns the caption buttons' grid columns so collapsed buttons leave no gap. Close is always anchored to the rightmost slot; the maximize/restore pair and minimize fill the remaining slots from the right.
member.param: The visibility of the minimize button.
The visibility of the maximize button.
The visibility of the restore button.
The visibility of the close button.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.HandleGetMinMaxInfo(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Clamps the maximized rectangle to the nearest monitor's work area in response to WM_GETMINMAXINFO: positions/sizes to the work area, applies the auto-hide-taskbar shift when the work area covers the whole monitor, and enforces the window's Min/Max width/height on the native resize track (DPI-scaled). Marks the message handled so the clamped values are honoured.
member.param: The window handle.
The message parameter.
Indicates whether the message was handled.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.HandleMaxButtonClick(System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Handles a click released over the snap-layout max/restore hit area (WM_NCLBUTTONUP with HTMAXBUTTON) by toggling the window state through the same direct path as the command handlers and refreshing the caption buttons.
member.param: Indicates whether the click was handled.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.HitTestTitleBar(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Maps a screen-space WM_NCHITTEST point to a hit-test result for the custom caption. Resolution order: the top resize band wins first; then the Windows 11 snap-layout flyout over the max/restore button (HTMAXBUTTON); minimize and close fall through to 0 so the WPF buttons fire; the remaining title-bar area returns HTCAPTION for dragging unless it is over interactive content or the window is not moveable.
member.param: The message parameter containing the screen-space point.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FluenceWindow.TryGetTopResizeHit(System.Windows.Point,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Resolves whether falls in the top resize band and, if so, which resize hit (HTTOP, HTTOPLEFT, or HTTOPRIGHT) applies. The band is suppressed when the window is maximized or not resizable.
member.param: The point to test, in window coordinates.
The resulting resize hit, if any.
member.returns: if the point falls in the top resize band; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.FlyoutBase
member.summary: Represents the base class for flyout controls that display lightweight UI in a light-dismiss anchored to a placement target, mirroring the WinUI 3 FlyoutBase contract.
member.remarks: The popup is created lazily on the first call with set to (clicking outside the flyout dismisses it). The popup uses a custom placement callback so the flyout is centered on the facing edge of its placement target, matching WinUI, and Escape pressed inside the flyout dismisses it. Derived classes supply the popup child via .

member.summary: Computes the custom popup placements that center a popup on the facing edge of its placement target, matching WinUI flyout positioning: horizontal centering for / and vertical centering for / . The opposite edge is offered as the fallback so the popup flips at screen edges like the native placement modes. Shared with ; internal so tests can verify the placement math directly.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.IsFooterItemProperty
member.summary: Internal inheritable attached flag marking the footer items region. The Top pane template sets it on PART_FooterItemsHost, so it inherits onto the footer s; the item template reads it to render those items icon-only in Top mode. The Left/LeftCompact templates do not set it, scoping the gear-only rule to Top mode. Inheritance (rather than a code marker) keeps the rule confined to Top without a per-item pane-mode binding. Not public API.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.UpdateBackButtonState(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Transitions the back button to the correct BackButtonStates VSM state based on . Called without transitions on initial template application; with transitions on runtime changes.
member.param: Indicates whether to use visual transitions.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.ApplyPaneColumnWidthOnTemplateApplied
member.summary: Sets the freshly templated pane column width during . When a Left <-> LeftCompact switch is in flight ( is set), the new column animates from the captured pre-swap width to its target, continuing the collapse/expand-style flight across the template swap; otherwise the width snaps.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.RefreshIndicators(System.Boolean,Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationViewItem)
member.summary: Repositions both the main and footer selection indicators. The selection model guarantees at most one region (main menu or footer) owns the selection, so at most one indicator shows.
member.param: Indicates whether to animate the indicator movement.
The previously selected item, if any.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.PositionFooterIndicator(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Snaps the footer selection indicator onto the selected footer item, or hides it when no footer item is selected. The footer region typically holds a single item, so the indicator does not fly laterally; instead it fades and scales in when a footer item becomes selected and out when it is deselected, matching the feel of the main region's arrive/depart.
member.param: Indicates whether to animate the indicator movement.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.AnimateFooterIndicatorVisibility(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Fades and scales the footer selection indicator in ( is ) or out, mirroring the main indicator's arrive/depart easing. When is the indicator snaps directly to the target opacity and scale. The scaled axis follows the indicator orientation: horizontal in Top mode, vertical otherwise.
member.param: Indicates whether the indicator is appearing or disappearing.
Indicates whether the navigation view is in top mode.
Indicates whether to animate the indicator visibility change.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.NavigationView.StopFooterAnimation
member.summary: Cancels any in-flight footer-indicator animations and bumps the generation guard so their completion callbacks no-op.

member.param: The navigation view item for which to calculate the indicator position.
The indicator element.
The host element containing the indicator.
Indicates whether the navigation view is in top mode.
member.returns: The calculated position for the indicator.

member.summary: Immediately places the main indicator at the target offset with no animation.

member.exception: Thrown if the selection indicator template part is missing.

The target position for the indicator.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager
member.summary: A page indicator mirroring the WinUI 3 PipsPager: a horizontal or vertical run of round pip dots, one per visible page, with the selected pip rendered larger in the accent fill. Clicking a pip selects its page, optional previous/next chevron buttons step the selection, and arrow keys move the selection while keyboard focus is inside the pager.

remarks.para: Pips are generated in code into the PART_PipsHost panel (the same approach as ). When exceeds , the pager shows a sliding window of pips centered on the selection where possible and clamped at the ends. Subscribe to to react to selection moves from any input path.

Two WinUI behaviors are deliberate v1 omissions: the scale-down animation of edge pips inside the scrolling viewport (the window simply re-renders), and WinUI's edge-scrolling window (which keeps the window still until the selection reaches its edge) in favor of the simpler centered window. Navigation buttons in mode collapse when the pointer leaves, so the pager's desired size changes with hover.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.#cctor
member.summary: Initializes static members of the PipsPager class and overrides the default style metadata so the control picks up its themed template from Generic.xaml.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.NumberOfPages
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of pages represented by the pager. Negative values coerce to 0. Default is 0 (no pips).

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.SelectedPageIndex
member.summary: Gets or sets the zero-based index of the selected page. Values coerce into [0, - 1], and to 0 while the pager has no pages. Binds two-way by default.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of pips realized at once. When exceeds this count, the pager shows a sliding window of this many pips centered on the selection where possible. Values below 1 coerce to 1. Default is 5, matching WinUI.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.Orientation
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the pips flow horizontally or vertically. The default template also swaps the navigation chevrons between left/right and up/down to match. Default is .

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.summary: Gets or sets when the previous-page chevron button is shown. The button is disabled while the first page is selected. Default is , matching WinUI.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.NextButtonVisibilityProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.NextButtonVisibility
member.summary: Gets or sets when the next-page chevron button is shown. The button is disabled while the last page is selected. Default is , matching WinUI.

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.SelectedIndexChanged
member.summary: Occurs after has changed from any input path (pip click, navigation buttons, arrow keys, or a programmatic set). The event args carry the previous and the new zero-based page index.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.PipsPager.UpdatePips
member.summary: Recomputes the visible pip window and the navigation button states. The host is rebuilt only when the window moved or resized; otherwise the realized pips just refresh their checked state. Keyboard focus follows the selected pip whenever it was inside the host, so arrow-key and click interaction stay coherent across window rebuilds.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class and subscribes to size changes for layout updates. Loaded and Unloaded are also wired so the repeat-forever indeterminate animation only runs while the control is in a live visual tree, mirroring .

member.summary: Raises on this control's automation peer so Narrator announces the error or paused state change without moving focus. Uses only net472-safe APIs (no RaiseNotificationEvent).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.OnLoaded(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Re-applies the resolved visual state when the control (re)enters a live visual tree, restarting the indeterminate animation that stopped.

The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.OnUnloaded(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Stops the repeat-forever indeterminate animation when the control leaves the visual tree so closed windows do not leak rooted animation clocks.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.UpdateIndicatorHostClip
member.summary: Clips the indicator host (and therefore the determinate fill and both indeterminate bars) to its rounded-rectangle geometry. WPF's clips only to the rectangular bounds, so the translating indeterminate bars (which overshoot both edges of the control) would otherwise show square ends where they cross the edge. A geometry clip matching keeps the accent conforming to the rounded indicator on every animation frame, exactly as the left-anchored determinate fill already does.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.SyncPrimitivesFromMode(Fluence.Wpf.ProgressBarMode)
member.summary: Maps the legacy alias onto the WinUI-orthogonal primitives (, , , and the internal step flag). The mapping is one-way: changing the primitives never writes back to , and suppresses the primitive callbacks so the resolver runs exactly once per mode change.
member.param: The progress bar mode to synchronize from.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.ApplyProgressMode
member.summary: Resolves the visual state from the three primitives (, , ): toggles determinate-versus-indeterminate element visibility, starts or stops the indeterminate animation, hides the baseline track while indeterminate, and applies the matching state brush to the fill and both indeterminate bars.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressBar.ApplyStateBrush
member.summary: Applies the state brush to the determinate fill and both indeterminate bars: critical when is set, caution when is set, otherwise the default accent fill.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ProgressRing
member.summary: A circular progress indicator that supports both determinate and indeterminate modes.

remarks.para: Indeterminate animation follows the WinUI 3 motion model: a private sweep-fraction dependency property pulses the arc length from zero to half the circumference and back over a 2 second linear cycle, while the template's PART_IndeterminateRotate transform spins the arc from 90 to 1170 degrees over the same 2 second cycle.
Determinate mode renders a stroked arc through ; the arc end-angle tweens for 367 ms with a WinUI-style decelerating key spline when changes.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.RatingControl
member.summary: A Fluent Design star-based rating control. Renders up to star glyphs using Segoe Fluent Icons (U+E734 StarEmpty / U+E735 StarFilled). Brush states: AccentFillColorDefaultBrush (filled), TextFillColorSecondaryBrush (unset), TextFillColorDisabledBrush (disabled).

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TabView
member.summary: A aligned with the WinUI 3 TabView: per-tab close buttons, a trailing "add" (+) button, horizontally scrollable tab strip with scroll navigation buttons, and WinUI-styled selection indicator.

remarks.para: produces containers by default. Consumers can bind to and optionally supply an to render header content for each item; the icon and close-button chrome are always supplied by the container template.
Listen to to create new tabs and to to remove a tab; this control does not itself mutate the items collection so applications remain in full control of their data model.
PART_ScrollBackButton and PART_ScrollForwardButton are automatically shown or hidden based on whether the tab strip overflows the available width.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip
member.summary: A contextual tip surface with a title, subtitle, body content, and optional action and close buttons, mirroring the WinUI 3 TeachingTip control. The tip is hosted in an internal light-weight : when is set the popup is anchored to it, centered on the target edge selected by ; untargeted tips dock to the bottom-right corner of the active window content (or center when is explicitly Center) and hide the beak. The body uses the inherited and .

While it is and is , an alternate X close button is shown in the top-right corner of the tip instead; while it is and light dismiss is enabled, the tip shows no close affordance at all.

The template positions the beak on the edge facing the target and hides it for .

member.summary: Occurs when the action button is invoked, before executes. Invoking the action button does not close the tip.

member.remarks: Escape pressed inside the open tip dismisses it, mirroring the WinUI keyboard contract. The close runs through the pipeline so is raised as usual.

member.summary: Raises on this control's automation peer so Narrator announces the tip content without moving focus when the tip opens. Uses only net472-safe APIs (no RaiseNotificationEvent).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.MapPlacementSide(Fluence.Wpf.TeachingTipPlacementMode)
member.summary: Maps the WinUI-style to the WPF popup side the tip opens on, feeding the shared edge-centering placement callback. maps to the bottom side; never reaches this mapping because centered tips use the native .
member.param: The requested tip placement.
member.returns: The equivalent popup side.

member.summary: Resolves to the concrete edge used for the popup placement and the beak position.
member.param: The preferred placement mode.
member.returns: The resolved placement mode.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.ResolveFallbackPlacementTarget
member.summary: Resolves the placement target for an untargeted tip: the content of the active window first, then the active window itself, then the application main window.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.OpenPopup
member.summary: Opens the host popup with the current placement and light-dismiss configuration.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.ClosePopup
member.summary: Closes the host popup; is raised from the popup's Closed event.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.ApplyPlacement(System.Windows.Controls.Primitives.Popup)
member.summary: Anchors the popup to , centered on the target edge selected by (via the shared edge-centering placement callback); explicit Center placement keeps the native centered popup. Untargeted tips dock to the bottom-right corner of the active window content per WinUI, or center when is explicitly Center, and record so the beak stays hidden. The resolved placement is recorded in .
member.param: The popup to which the placement is being applied.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.GetEdgePlacements(System.Windows.Size,System.Windows.Size,System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: The popup's custom placement callback for targeted tips: centers the tip on the target edge selected by , sharing the math with the flyout family.
member.param: The size of the popup.
The size of the target element.
The offset to apply to the placement.
member.returns: An array of custom popup placements.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.GetBottomRightPlacements(System.Windows.Size,System.Windows.Size,System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: The popup's custom placement callback for untargeted tips: docks the tip to the bottom-right corner of the fallback placement target (the active window content), matching the WinUI default position for untargeted teaching tips.
member.param: The size of the popup.
The size of the target element.
The offset to apply to the placement.
member.returns: An array of custom popup placements.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.EnsurePopup
member.summary: Creates the host popup on first use and re-hosts the tip as the popup child, mirroring how hosts its presenter. A tip declared inside a panel is detached from that panel before it becomes the popup child.
member.returns: The popup that hosts the tip.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TeachingTip.DetachFromParent(System.Windows.FrameworkElement)
member.summary: Detaches from its current parent so it can become the popup child. The logical parent is preferred because it owns the content slot (a ContentControl's content reports a ContentPresenter as its visual parent); the visual parent is the fallback for template-generated hosts. Unsupported parents fail fast instead of letting the popup throw WPF's generic re-parenting error.
member.param: The element to detach.
member.exception: The parent is not a , a (which includes Border), or a .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TextBox.AnnounceLiveRegion
member.summary: Raises on the helper-text element so Narrator announces the current validation message without moving focus. The helper text (whose is the message and which declares in the template) is the correct target; the control falls back to its own peer only when the part is unavailable. Uses only net472-safe APIs (no RaiseNotificationEvent).

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TextBox._lastAnnouncedMessage
member.summary: Tracks the last validation message for which a live-region announcement was raised. An announcement is re-raised when the message changes even if the state did not.

The effective message text that would be announced.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker
member.summary: A control that lets the user pick a time of day from hour, minute, and (in 12-hour mode) AM/PM selector columns hosted in a light-dismiss flyout, mirroring the WinUI 3 TimePicker. The always-visible field is a button-styled row showing the selected hour, two-digit minute, and culture AM/PM designator; the flyout commits the pending column selection through its accept button and discards it through cancel.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.TwelveHourClock
member.summary: The value selecting the 12-hour clock with an AM/PM designator column.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.TwentyFourHourClock
member.summary: The value selecting the 24-hour clock without a designator column.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.#cctor
member.summary: Initializes static members of the TimePicker class and overrides the default style metadata so the control picks up its themed template from Generic.xaml.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.SelectedTimeProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.SelectedTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the currently selected time of day, or when no time has been picked yet. Changing this property raises .

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.ClockIdentifierProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.ClockIdentifier
member.summary: Gets or sets the clock system used by the field and the hour column: "12HourClock" (hours 1..12 plus an AM/PM designator column) or "24HourClock" (hours 0..23, no designator column). Any other value is coerced back to "12HourClock". The default follows the user's regional clock: "24HourClock" when the current culture's short time pattern uses the 24-hour 'H' specifier, otherwise "12HourClock". An explicitly set value always wins over the regional default.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.MinuteIncrementProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.MinuteIncrement
member.summary: Gets or sets the step between the offered minute values (for example 15 offers 00, 15, 30, and 45). Values are clamped into 1..59.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.HeaderProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.Header
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional header content shown above the field.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.PlaceholderTextProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.PlaceholderText
member.summary: Gets or sets the placeholder text shown in the field while is (for example "Pick a time").

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.SelectedTimeChanged
member.summary: Occurs after changes, whether through the flyout's accept button or a programmatic update.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetDefaultClockIdentifier
member.summary: Computes the regional default for : "24HourClock" when the current culture's short time pattern uses the 24-hour 'H' specifier, otherwise "12HourClock". Captured once as the dependency property's default metadata value; explicitly set values always win.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.CoerceClockIdentifier(System.Windows.DependencyObject,System.Object)
member.summary: Coerces to one of its two supported values, falling back to the 12-hour clock for anything unrecognized.
member.param: The dependency object on which the property is being set.
The proposed value for the property.
member.returns: The coerced value for the property.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.CoerceMinuteIncrement(System.Windows.DependencyObject,System.Object)
member.summary: Clamps into the supported 1..59 range.
member.param: The dependency object on which the property is being set.
The proposed value for the property.
member.returns: The coerced value for the property.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetTwelveHourDisplayHour(System.Int32)
member.summary: Maps an hour of day to its 12-hour display value, where both midnight (hour 0) and noon (hour 12) show as 12.
member.param: The hour of day to map.
member.returns: The 12-hour display value.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetAmDesignator(System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Returns the culture's AM designator, falling back to the invariant "AM" when the culture provides none. On .NET Framework NLS many locales (de-DE, fr-FR, sv-SE, it-IT) report empty designators, which would otherwise render two blank, indistinguishable period values.
member.param: The culture whose AM designator is being retrieved.
member.returns: The AM designator for the specified culture.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetPmDesignator(System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Returns the culture's PM designator, falling back to the invariant "PM" when the culture provides none. See for the .NET Framework NLS rationale.
member.param: The culture whose PM designator is being retrieved.
member.returns: The PM designator for the specified culture.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetPeriodDesignator(System.Int32,System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Returns the current culture's AM or PM designator for an hour of day, with the invariant "AM"/"PM" fallback for cultures whose designators are empty.
member.param: The hour of day to get the period designator for.
The culture whose period designator is being retrieved.
member.returns: The period designator for the specified hour and culture.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.ScrollSelectionIntoView(System.Windows.Controls.Primitives.Selector)
member.summary: Scrolls the selector's current selection into view when it is a ListBox.
member.param: The selector whose selection should be scrolled into view.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.IsWithinLightDismissReopenLockout
member.summary: Returns whether a flyout-button click arrived within the short lockout after a light dismiss. Clicking the open field light-dismisses the StaysOpen=false popup on the mouse press, and without this guard the same click would immediately reopen it, so the field could never close its own flyout.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.OnPopupClosed(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Records when the popup closes through a light dismiss (any close that did not run through ), arming the reopen lockout consumed by . Accept, cancel, and Escape closes do not arm it, so programmatic close-and-reopen flows stay instant.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.OnPopupPreviewKeyDown(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles flyout keyboard interaction at the popup root: Escape discards the pending column selection (the cancel-button path) and Enter commits it (the accept-button path). Enter is left alone while a flyout command button has keyboard focus so the button's native click handling wins.
member.param: The source of the event.
The event data.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.MoveFocusIntoPopup
member.summary: Moves keyboard focus to the selected item of the first visible selector column once the open flyout has laid out, so the flyout is immediately keyboard operable.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetFirstVisibleSelectorColumn
member.summary: Returns the first visible selector column in display order (hour, minute, AM/PM); the AM/PM column is collapsed by the template trigger in 24-hour mode.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.PopulateSelectorColumns
member.summary: Fills the selector columns from (or the current time of day when unset), snapping the minute down to the nearest step, and highlights the matching items. The columns are plain scrollable lists; WinUI's infinitely looping selectors are a deliberate v1 omission.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.CommitPendingSelection
member.summary: Commits the pending hour, minute, and AM/PM column values into .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetPendingHour
member.summary: Returns the pending hour of day (0..23). In 12-hour mode the display hour and the AM/PM column combine so that 12 AM maps to hour 0 and 12 PM maps to hour 12.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.GetPendingIsPm
member.summary: Returns whether the pending AM/PM column selection is PM, falling back to the populated base time when the column has no selection.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.UpdateFieldText
member.summary: Writes the selected time into the field segments: the hour per , the minute always two-digit, and the culture's AM/PM designator in 12-hour mode. Out-of-range values (negative spans, spans past a day) are normalized into a valid hour and minute of day with the same math as , so the field can never display an hour such as "-1". The placeholder swap for a null and the designator collapse in 24-hour mode are handled by template triggers.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.TimePicker.IsTwentyFourHourClock
member.summary: Gets whether selects the 24-hour clock.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleButton
member.summary: A Fluent Design styled ToggleButton with accent checked state.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleButton.#cctor
member.summary: Initializes static members of the ToggleButton class and overrides the default style metadata.
member.remarks: This static constructor ensures that the ToggleButton control uses its own style by default, rather than inheriting the style from its base class. This is important for custom control theming in WPF.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleButton.CornerRadiusProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleButton.AppearanceProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.summary: Gets or sets the corner radius of the toggle button.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleButton.Appearance
member.summary: Gets or sets the visual appearance of the toggle button.
member.remarks: The default template renders the single canonical WinUI toggle visual for every value: the checked state is the accent state, so a separate accent rest variant would make checked and unchecked indistinguishable. The property exists primarily for derived controls such as , which consume it in their own templates.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton
member.summary: A whose primary half toggles a checked state instead of firing a plain action: clicking it flips and then raises , while the secondary "chevron" half still opens the flyout. The canonical WinUI 3 ToggleSplitButton pattern, used when the primary half switches a mode on or off and the flyout chooses which variant of that mode applies.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.#cctor
member.summary: Initializes static members of the ToggleSplitButton class and overrides the default style metadata.
member.remarks: This static constructor ensures that the ToggleSplitButton control uses its custom style by associating the control with its default style key. This enables the control to be styled appropriately in XAML themes.

member.name: F:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.IsCheckedProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.IsChecked
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the toggle split button is checked. A primary-half click flips this value before is raised.

member.name: E:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.IsCheckedChanged
member.summary: Raised when changes, whether from a primary-half click, the Toggle automation pattern, a binding, or a direct property set.
member.remarks: Unlike WinUI, the event also fires for values applied before the control is loaded (for example markup-set initial values), matching how the WPF raises its Checked and Unchecked events.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.OnPrimaryButtonClick(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Toggles and then runs the inherited click behavior, mirroring the WinUI ToggleSplitButton primary-click contract: a handler observes the already-flipped state.
member.param: The primary button template part that raised the click.
The routed event data from the primary button.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.Toggle
member.summary: Flips without clearing bindings or consumer-set local values.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy
member.summary: Pure-logic policy layer for . All methods are stateless and side-effect-free so they can be unit tested without a window handle. The class maps the requested and OS capabilities to concrete DWM instructions and WPF parameters, insulating the window code-behind from the capability-detection and downgrade rules.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.CreateWindowChrome
member.summary: Constructs a canonical for a . The chrome is fixed regardless of backdrop or resize mode; per-state adjustments are made afterward via and .
member.remarks:
Parameter choices:
CaptionHeight = 0 Routes all title-bar hits through WM_NCHITTEST so the custom caption can distinguish drag, resize, snap, and button regions.
GlassFrameThickness = -1 Extends DWM glass into the full client area; overridden per-window by when no backdrop is active.
ResizeBorderThickness = 4 Matches the WinUI 3 / .NET 10 WPF FluentWindow invisible hit margin.
UseAeroCaptionButtons = false Disables the native Aero caption buttons; Fluence renders its own.
NonClientFrameEdges = None Lets the client area extend into the caption strip.
CornerRadius = 0 WPF-side rounding is off; rounded corners are driven by DWMWA_WINDOW_CORNER_PREFERENCE to avoid clipping the DWM backdrop.
member.returns: A new with the canonical Fluence settings.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.GetGlassFrameThickness(Fluence.Wpf.BackdropType,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the appropriate for the given backdrop and shadow state.
member.remarks: When a DWM backdrop is active (, , , or ), the thickness is -1 so DWM extends the glass into the client area and the backdrop shows through. The same -1 is used when the caller requests a drop shadow without a backdrop, because the shadow is rendered via the DWM glass frame. When neither is active the thickness is a very-thin-but-nonzero value (0.00001) so the resize border continues to hit-test while 's renderer does not paint a visible glass-frame artifact. This dual-path is intentional: setting -1 with on Windows 11 renders a visible glass artifact, so the tiny nonzero value is the correct prevention mechanism.
member.param: The requested (not necessarily effective) backdrop type.
when is set, requiring the DWM glass frame for shadow rendering even without a system backdrop.
member.returns: The glass-frame thickness to assign to .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.GetResizeBorderThickness(System.Windows.WindowState,System.Windows.ResizeMode)
member.summary: Returns the appropriate for the given window state and resize mode.
member.remarks: A maximized window has no visible resize handles; using 4 dp would let the border bleed over the taskbar or adjacent monitors. and forbid drag-resize so their hit margin is also zero.
member.param: The current .
The current .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.BuildFramePlan(System.Windows.WindowState,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities,System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Computes the that governs the window border appearance.
member.remarks:
The plan has two independent halves:
WPF-template border
Active window with accent borders enabled gets a 2 dp border keyed to SystemAccentColorBrush. Inactive windows revert to CardStrokeColorDefaultSolidBrush. Maximized windows get a 0-thick border in every state.
DWM border color
When the OS supports DWMWA_BORDER_COLOR and the window is active with accent borders, the COLORREF derived from is emitted. Otherwise is used, which tells DWM to restore its own border.
member.param: The current .
when is the foreground window.
when ApplicationAccentColorManager.IsAccentColorOnTitleBarsEnabled is set.
The OS capability snapshot.
The current system accent color.
member.returns: A describing the border to apply.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.ResolveEffectiveBackdrop(Fluence.Wpf.BackdropType,Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities)
member.summary: Resolves the that will actually be applied after downgrading for OS capability gaps.
member.remarks:
Downgrade rules:
Auto / Mica
Resolves to when either DWMWA_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE (22H2+) or the legacy DWMWA_MICA_EFFECT (21H2) is available; falls back to on Windows 10.
Acrylic / Tabbed
Passes through on 22H2+. Downgrades to Mica on pre-22H2 Win11 (only DWMWA_MICA_EFFECT is available there). Downgrades to None on Windows 10.
None Never upgraded, regardless of OS capabilities.
member.param: The requested by the caller.
The OS capability snapshot.
member.returns: The effective to apply.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Controls.WindowPolicy.BuildBackdropPlan(Fluence.Wpf.BackdropType,Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationTheme,Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities,System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Builds the complete for a window from the requested backdrop type, the current theme, and the OS capability snapshot.
member.remarks:
The plan is computed in one pass:
downgrades the request for the current OS.
None effective backdrop gets a solid fallback background and for the caption. On 22H2+ the plan emits to explicitly clear any previous Mica or Acrylic; on Windows 10 no DWMWA_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE write is attempted.
Mica on pre-22H2 Win11 uses the legacy DWMWA_MICA_EFFECT path ( = ), never the canonical DWMWA_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE.
Any other active backdrop maps to a DWMSBT_* value via .
member.param: The requested by the caller.
The resolved application theme (used for immersive dark mode).
The OS capability snapshot.
The opaque background color to use when no DWM backdrop is active.
member.returns: A describing all DWM writes to perform.

member.summary: Boosts saturation in HLS color space by the given factor (capped at 1.0). Matches winaccent's increase_saturation which uses Python colorsys HLS (not HSV).

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.HsvColorHelper.GenerateAccentRampWinaccent(System.Windows.Media.Color,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.Windows.Media.Color@)
member.summary:
Generates a six-shade accent ramp from a base color by applying lightness deltas in HSL space with saturation pinned to the chroma ceiling. For achromatic inputs (HSL S near zero), the base is returned unchanged for every stop so greys do not suddenly grow saturation.
Deltas are spaced ~10-12% apart on the L axis so adjacent stops (e.g. Light1 next to the base, or Dark1 next to the base) are perceptibly distinct - controls that reference different rungs of the ramp for hover / pressed / focus states then show visible variation. An earlier calibration (Candidate F, fitted against 21 OS-captured AccentPalette ramps) used much tighter near-base steps (Light1 +7%, Dark1 -4%) which approximated the OS perceptual transform but made adjacent stops nearly indistinguishable in control templates. We do not mirror the OS projection of arbitrary user input into the Fluent-compatible subspace - by design we use the user-supplied base verbatim - so the wider spread here is the right tradeoff for usable control highlights. See KNOWN_ISSUES.md "OS- transform modeling for the accent ramp" for the broader rationale.
member.param: The base color for the accent ramp.
The first light shade.
The second light shade.
The third light shade.
The first dark shade.
The second dark shade.
The third dark shade.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.HsvColorHelper.ShiftHslMaxSat(System.Windows.Media.Color,System.Double)
member.summary: Shifts a color's HSL lightness by with saturation pinned to the chroma ceiling (1.0). Achromatic inputs (S < 0.05) are returned unchanged so greys do not develop hue.
member.param: The base color to adjust.
The delta to apply to the lightness.
member.returns: The adjusted color.

member.summary: Converts to HSL space (Hue in degrees [0..360], Saturation and Lightness in [0..1]).
member.param: The color to convert.

The resulting saturation.
The resulting lightness.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.SnapLayoutHelper
member.summary: Probes the per-user Windows 11 Snap Layout flyout preference. The flyout appears when the user hovers a window's maximize button and Windows reports the hit-region as HTMAXBUTTON. Without the preference check, returning HTMAXBUTTON shows the flyout even for users who disabled it in Settings.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.SnapLayoutHelper.IsSnapLayoutEnabled
member.summary: Returns when the user has the Windows 11 Snap Layout flyout enabled. Defaults to when the registry value is absent (the Windows 11 default), so the flyout is shown unless the user explicitly turned it off.
member.remarks: The preference is stored under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced as EnableSnapAssistFlyout (DWORD). A missing value, a non-DWORD value, or any value other than 0 is treated as enabled.
member.returns: when the Snap Layout flyout is enabled or not configured; when the user has explicitly disabled it.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities.SupportsRoundedCorners
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the OS supports the DWM rounded-corner preference (DWMWA_WINDOW_CORNER_PREFERENCE, attribute 33). on any Windows 11 build (build 22000+); on Windows 10.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities.SupportsCaptionColor
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the OS supports setting the DWM caption color via DWMWA_CAPTION_COLOR (attribute 35). on any Windows 11 build; on Windows 10.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities.SupportsBorderColor
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the OS supports setting the DWM border color via DWMWA_BORDER_COLOR (attribute 34). on any Windows 11 build; on Windows 10.

member.name: P:Fluence.Wpf.Helpers.WindowCapabilities.Current
member.summary: Gets a snapshot for the current OS build by delegating to , which caches the OS version after the first probe. Always returns a non-null instance; Windows 10 builds get a snapshot where all capability flags are .

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeConstants
member.summary: Compile-time Win32, DWM, UxTheme, and shell constants shared across the native interop layer. Values are the documented operating-system ordinals and bit flags; they are pinned by the interop tests, so changing one here is a wire-contract break, not a refactor.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods
member.summary: The native interop surface for and its policy/capability helpers: DWM backdrop and frame attributes, UxTheme caption suppression, immersive color queries, monitor and taskbar geometry, layered-window presentation, and the RtlGetVersion OS-build probe. Every method is best-effort and handle-safe so it can be called from a presentation path without throwing.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.GetImmersiveDarkModeAttribute(System.Int32)
member.summary: Selects the DWM immersive dark-mode window attribute id for a given OS build. The attribute moved from (19) to (20) starting at Windows 10 build 18362 (version 1903). Builds 17763..18361 (1809 era) must use 19, or the dark caption silently fails to apply. This selector is pure so it can be unit tested without a window handle.
member.param: The OS build number (for example 18362).
member.returns: The DWM attribute id to pass to .

to request the dark caption.
