member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.SetWindowCloak(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Cloaks or uncloaks a window via . While cloaked, DWM keeps the window fully composed off-screen and does not present it. Retained as part of the interop contract; deliberately does not cloak (its first-paint flash is solved by clearing the redirection surface), so the never-cloak invariant is asserted by the harden tests via . Any caller that does cloak MUST guarantee a matching uncloak; a window left cloaked is invisible.
member.param: The target window handle.
to cloak, to uncloak.
member.returns: on success.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.GetWindowCloakedState(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Reads the read-only attribute, returning the reason flags for why the window is cloaked. Zero means the window is not cloaked. Returns zero on any failure (for example when DWM composition is disabled).
member.param: The target window handle.
member.returns: The cloak reason flags, or zero when not cloaked or on failure.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.ColorToColorRef(System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Packs a into the 0x00BBGGRR COLORREF layout that DWM color attributes such as expect; the alpha channel is ignored. Despite the historical "ABGR" naming, the byte order produced here is COLORREF, so callers must not reuse it for an attribute that genuinely expects ABGR with a meaningful alpha channel.
member.param: The source color.
member.returns: The packed COLORREF value.

member.summary: Strips WS_SYSMENU from the window style so the native caption (and its buttons) stops painting over the custom Fluent caption.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.MinimizeWindowNative(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Minimizes a window through the native ShowWindow API. Used as a belt-and-braces fallback from the custom caption's minimize handler so minimize is guaranteed to work even when the chrome has stripped WS_SYSMENU/WS_MINIMIZEBOX (which blocks SC_MINIMIZE via DefWindowProc), the window is NoResize, topmost, or shown via ShowDialog() inside a nested dispatcher frame. ShowWindow honors SW_MINIMIZE regardless of window styles, so it cannot be silently gated the way WM_SYSCOMMAND can.
member.param: The target window handle.
member.returns: when the window is (or becomes) minimized.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Native.NativeMethods.MaximizeWindowNative(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Maximizes a window through the native ShowWindow API.
member.param: The target window handle.
member.returns: when the window is (or becomes) maximized.

member.summary: Provides a synchronization object for thread-safe operations within the containing class.

member.remarks: A tick is equal to 100 nanoseconds. This constant can be used to implement debouncing logic, ensuring that actions are not performed more frequently than the specified interval.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.BaseColorTables
member.summary: Loads per-theme Color tokens from the XAML resource dictionaries. The few theme-independent tokens (the Windows close-button brand colors) are seeded in code by ; only the per-theme Color-only tables are read from XAML. Brushes are built entirely in C# by and .

member.summary: Returns a dictionary of Color keys for the given , combining shared tokens and per-theme overrides.
member.param: The theme to load.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.BaseColorTables.AddSharedColors(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Windows.Media.Color})
member.summary: Seeds the theme-independent Color tokens that are identical across Light, Dark, and HighContrast. These are the canonical Windows close-button brand colors - the shell uses the same hover and pressed red and the white foreground in every theme - so they are defined here in code rather than duplicated across the per-theme XAML tables. Seeded before the per-theme table so a future theme could still override them.
member.param: The color table to seed.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.BrushFactory
member.summary: Builds a from a color map, publishing each Color token and a frozen twin (key + "Brush") for every entry.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.BrushFactory.Build(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Windows.Media.Color})
member.summary: Returns a containing every Color token and its corresponding frozen from . Color keys in the suppress-auto-twin list are published as Color tokens only; their brush twin uses an irregular name and is emitted by .
member.param: The dictionary of color tokens to build brushes for.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.ColorMap
member.summary: Builds a complete color map for a given theme and accent palette by merging base theme colors with all accent-derived values. This is the single place where every accent-derived Color token is computed; the engine then overlays these values (and their brush twins) on top of the authored base dictionaries.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.ColorMap.Build(Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationTheme,Fluence.Wpf.Theming.AccentPalette,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Builds a mapping every canonical Color key to its resolved for the given and accent palette.
member.param: The resolved concrete theme.
The resolved accent ramp.

When , the title-bar/window-border tokens are set to their machine-independent theme defaults (the no-color-prevalence values) and no registry, DWM, or OS-version probe is performed. Used by so the design-time snapshot is byte stable across machines. The live pipeline calls with the default (), preserving the registry-driven chrome behavior.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine
member.summary: The single-pipeline theme engine that resolves theme and accent intent into a computed and publishes it into application resources. The public facades (ApplicationThemeManager, ApplicationAccentColorManager) are thin wrappers that delegate to this engine: their Apply/ApplySystemAccent entry points call , and they raise their own public events by subscribing to .

member.summary: Gets the most recently resolved .

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine.BuildComputedDictionary(Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationTheme,Fluence.Wpf.Theming.AccentPalette)
member.summary: Builds the single computed dictionary published at slot [0] entirely in C#. Base Color tokens are read (Color entries only) from the per-theme and shared XAML via ; accent-derived Colors are computed by ; produces the solid brush twin for every Color token; and adds the non-twin brushes (elevation gradients, High-Contrast SystemColors brushes, ScrollBar track, accent overrides) plus the shared layout/shadow/focus tokens. No brush XAML is merged.
member.param: The application theme to use.
The accent palette to use.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine.BuildStandalone(Fluence.Wpf.ApplicationTheme)
member.summary: Builds the computed color + brush for using the default Windows accent (#0078D4), without publishing into application resources and without reading , the registry, or DWM. The default accent is forced through with an intent (the custom path runs the HSV ramp generator directly and never touches the registry or DwmGetColorizationParameters), and the title-bar/window-border tokens use their machine-independent theme defaults (deterministicChrome). The result is therefore deterministic and headless-safe, suitable for serializing a static design-time snapshot.
member.remarks: Runs the same -> -> sequence as the live pipeline so the snapshot stays faithful to runtime. It deliberately omits AcrylicNoiseBrush (a runtime-generated ), which the live appends after the fact. Only and are supported; high contrast is out of scope for design-time previews.
member.param: The application theme to use.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine.Publish(System.Windows.ResourceDictionary)
member.summary: Publishes into application resources.
member.param: The computed dictionary to publish.
member.returns: if the dictionary was actually published; if was null and the call was a no-op.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine.SetDeterministicChromeForTesting(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Test-only switch that forces the live publish pipeline to emit machine-independent title-bar / window-border chrome (the deterministic branch). The golden-parity snapshot was captured with color-prevalence OFF; without this the rebuild would read live OS personalization (HKCU DWM ColorPrevalence / AccentColor) and drift on machines that show the accent color on title bars.
member.param: Whether to enable deterministic chrome for testing.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.FluenceThemeEngine.ResetForTesting
member.summary: Resets engine state for test isolation.

member.name: T:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.SpecialBrushes
member.summary: Constructs every brush that is not a plain solid twin of a Color token, plus the theme-independent layout/shadow/focus tokens consumed by control templates. This is the single C# home for the special-brush definitions (elevation gradients, focus visuals, and high-contrast SystemColors aliases) that are not the plain solid twins BrushFactory emits for each Color key. All produced brushes are frozen.

member.summary: Builds the canonical Fluent elevation gradient: absolute mapping, 0,0 -> 0,3, flipped vertically about its centre, with stops at 0.33 () and 1.0 ().
member.param: The color for the 0.33 stop.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.SpecialBrushes.AddHighContrastBrushes(System.Windows.ResourceDictionary)
member.summary: High-contrast brush overrides snapshot live members, reproducing the non-accent brush overrides in Section 2 of Theme.HighContrast.xaml. Accent-derived brushes are intentionally left as their computed twins (the legacy C# accent overlay took precedence over the HC XAML for those keys, so the golden records the computed palette, not the HC SystemColors). A re-Apply on WM_SETTINGCHANGE refreshes the snapshot when the HC variant changes.
member.param: The resource dictionary to populate.

member.name: M:Fluence.Wpf.Theming.SpecialBrushes.AddSystemColorAliases(System.Windows.ResourceDictionary)
member.summary: SystemColor aliases consumed by the WinUI Gallery color guidance page. These resolve to live in every theme (bound to SystemColors keys unconditionally).
member.param: The resource dictionary to populate.
